message:
fix(web): add WebGPU guard to prevent WASM loading on unsupported browsers

The standalone /web demo's module script was attempting to import() WASM
modules even on browsers without WebGPU (e.g. iOS Safari), causing
"Importing a module script failed" errors. Added an early navigator.gpu
check that throws before any fetch/import runs, letting the existing
fallback UI show cleanly.

Also added corresponding injection step to sync-showcase.sh so the
guard survives future re-syncs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -137,6 +137,16 @@ if (!navigator.gpu) {\
 
     echo "Injected WebGPU fallback page into index.html"
   fi
+
+  # 3. Inject WebGPU guard at top of main module script to prevent WASM loading on unsupported browsers
+  if ! grep -q 'WebGPU guard' "${DEST}index.html"; then
+    sed -i '/<script type="module">/a\
+// ── WebGPU guard — bail out immediately on unsupported browsers ────────\
+if (!navigator.gpu) {\
+  throw new Error("WebGPU not supported — skipping WASM demo initialization.");\
+}' "${DEST}index.html"
+    echo "Injected WebGPU guard into module script"
+  fi
 fi
 
 # Summary and version manifest
